purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the hci_sync module of the Bluetooth stack. The bug is caused by calling hci_unregister_dev after HCI_UNREGISTER flag has been set. This can lead to use-after-free errors.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c. This is a perfective commit because it improves the quality of the code by removing unnecessary includes.
The commit updates documentation for a store metric named 'N/A'. It adds this new metric to the report table.
The commit fixes a bug in hugetlb handling by introducing vma locks for sharable huge page mappings. It also prevents potential bugs related to pmd sharing and unmapping of huge pages.

The commit adds two new properties to a YAML file related to the Realtek RT5682S codec. These properties are AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply which represent regulators supplying power for analog signals and microphone bias respectively.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call. The leak was present because of the error handling path that did not release a resource.

The commit removes an unused field 'work' from a struct named 'mlx5e_async_ctx'. This struct is used in the context of kernel thread local storage (kTLS) and appears to be related to asynchronous operations.
The commit changes a struct member from unsigned to signed in cfg80211.h file. It seems to be related to handling of keys in wireless networking.
The commit is changing a configuration file in Linux kernel for RISC-V architecture. It removes a specific platform from the list of supported platforms.

The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() and uses it in place of an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode. It also modifies the initialization of FIFOs based on whether the chip is in listen-only or CAN-FD mode.
The commit is about skipping tests when a Real-Time Clock (RTC) device is not present. It modifies the selftests for RTC to handle this case.
The commit removes an unnecessary assignment of a variable 'bytes' which is never read.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks in a Linux kernel configuration. It does this by modifying Kconfig files and source code.
The commit is a refactoring of the code in 'arch/x86/kvm/mmu/spte.h'. It removes duplicated 'be split' from the file.
The commit reverts a change that populates a clock table based on DCFCLK values. The purpose of this change is to prevent an underflow situation when DCFCLK states are disabled.
The commit adds a new compatible string for Renesas's R9A07G054 SoC to the Mali Bifrost GPU in the device tree bindings.

The commit changes various files related to PCIe drivers in Linux kernel. It appears to be a refactoring effort to replace the old 'pcie_port' structure with the new 'dw_pcie_rp' structure. This change is likely part of an ongoing effort to modernize and streamline the design of these drivers.
The commit fixes a bug in the code related to printing of protocol values. The warning message indicates that the format specifier used for printing the protocol value is incorrect.
The commit fixes a bug in the jump_label.c file where an unsigned integer comparison was being made against a signed integer. This could potentially lead to incorrect behavior or crashes.
The commit removes a hardware variable from SetHwReg8188EU() function in rtw_p2p.c file. This change is part of the ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit changes a variable declaration from non-static to static in the file arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c. This change is necessary because of sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.
The commit fixes a bug in the MDSS interrupt provider by removing an incorrectly specified interrupt flags cell on MDSS nodes.
The commit allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref. This is done to prepare for allowing changes to the array size from a driver.
The commit adds new PMICs and SoCs to a socinfo driver in the QCOM platform.
The commit changes a switch-case statement to an if-else statement in rtl8xxxu_core.c file. The change improves the code by making it more readable and easier to understand.
The commit removes a variable 'is_enabled' from the clk-prcmu.c file which is used to keep track of whether the clock is enabled or not.
The commit is about refactoring the code in 'arch/riscv/mm/init.c'. It moves a macro to an adjacent #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL and groups first type usage cases into one.

The commit enables two new DMA (Direct Memory Access) controllers for GPI (General Purpose Input/Output) and QUP (Quick Universal Processor). These are hardware components that can be used to improve the performance of I2C nodes in the future.

The commit fixes a typo in comments, improves code readability by ensuring that each comment is on its own line, and replaces an unclear comment with one that better explains the purpose of a variable.

The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the release_swfw_sync function of igc_i225.c file. The infinite loop occurs when failing to acquire the HW semaphore, which is needed for resource release. It typically happens if the hardware is surprise-removed. In this case, there's nothing to do but log an error and quit.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use more modern terminology for clocking.
The commit removes a pointless hack that was previously used to check if a file is a swap file. The hack was unnecessary because the IS_SWAPFILE macro is always defined.

The commit introduces a new feature to the GTP (GPRS Tunneling Protocol) device driver in Linux kernel. It adds support for sending an echo response when it receives an echo request from a peer. This is done to comply with the GSM TS 09.60 and 3GPP TS 29.281 specifications, which require that a GTP device responds to echo requests.

The commit replaces 'next' version of cpumask functions with 'first' version where appropriate. This change is aimed at improving the code efficiency by replacing potentially inefficient operations with more efficient ones.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. This is done to prevent an error message from being displayed when a STA roams between two access points.
The commit adds support for a new platform 'adl_rt5682' without an amplifier in the sound/soc/intel/boards/sof_rt5682.c file.
The commit is adding a check to ensure that zerocopy send requests for protocols that don't support it fail.

The commit modifies a LaTeX style file to load the xeCJK package only if it exists and the Noto Sans CJK SC font is available. This is done to prevent errors in xelatex when using translations that require Asian fonts.
The commit is adding a case macro to checkpatch script which allows 'case' macros in certain contexts.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in the drm/tiny/simpledrm.c file. The primary purpose of this change is to improve the architecture and code sharing among drivers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding worker in MT8195. It also introduces three functions to manage the hardware resources used by the decoder.
The commit removes pointless VPRINTK() calls from libata-core.c file in the ATA driver. It also removes ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function call which is not used anymore.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in the AMD GPU driver. It modifies the code to handle extended blank periods when entering Freesync mode.
The commit updates a function pointer prototype in kgd_kfd_interface.h to match reality so that there is no more CFI violation.

The commit corrects a typo in the 'stm32-pinctrl.yaml' file. It changes the property name from 'reset' to 'resets'. This is done to ensure that the binding is correctly used and does not cause any errors.
The commit is a bug fix for an array out-of-bounds warning in the ath9k driver code.
The commit changes a function call from pm_runtime_get_sync to pm_runtime_resume_and_get. This is done to simplify the code.

The commit moves the installation and removal of fixed files from io_uring to filetable.c, which is where it belongs. It also modifies how these operations are performed by taking a ctx rather than an io_kiocb.
The commit updates the device tree bindings for NXP's IMX8MQ VPU. It splits the original binding into two separate ones, one for each decoder block (G1 and G2).
The commit removes a macro named IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON from the iwlwifi driver's configuration file. This macro is used in one place within the same file.
